name: MD&A Linguistic Tone Shift as Forward Return Signal
mechanism: Management Discussion & Analysis (MD&A) sections encode management's true outlook through linguistic choices. Increased hedging and decreased certainty in MD&A relative to prior filings signals deteriorating business conditions before they appear in financial numbers. The LLM advantage: systematic cross-sectional comparison of linguistic features (hedge density, certainty markers, readability) across thousands of 10-K filings — something no human analyst can do consistently.
llm_advantage: Deterministic linguistic feature extraction (hedge density, certainty markers, readability scores) at scale across all 10-K/10-Q filings. No single analyst reads every filing.
why_underweighted: Linguistic analysis of SEC filings requires processing millions of text documents. Most quant funds focus on numerical data. NLP-based strategies are still nascent in retail-accessible form.

known_biases: Only covers 10-K annual filings (not 10-Q interim updates)
Filing text extraction may miss tables and exhibits
Section boundary detection heuristic — may occasionally include adjacent sections
Survivorship bias for stocks delisted before ~2017

falsifiable_prediction: Companies in the top quartile of MD&A hedge density increase should underperform bottom quartile by at least 300bps annualized over the subsequent 3 months.
biggest_weakness: MD&A language changes may be reactive rather than predictive — management may change language AFTER results deteriorate, not before. Also, 10-K frequency (annual) severely limits sample size.
